A test script to assert strict equality between two uninitialized variables.

# Purpose
This code is a simple script that uses an assertion to check if two variables, `x` and `y`, are strictly equal. The variables `x` and `y` are declared but not initialized, which means they both have the value `undefined` by default. The `assert` function is used to verify that `x` and `y` are strictly equal (`===`), which will be true since both are `undefined`. This script provides narrow functionality, primarily serving as a basic test or demonstration of variable declaration and strict equality comparison in JavaScript.
